commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Buffles, which cannot be tamed. Hebrew, "with oxen. "We must understand, on rocks. (Calmet)
Septuagint, "Shall they be silent when they are with females? "(Haydock)
To turn the works of justice into sins, is no less unnatural than to plough with wild buffles. (Worthington)

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: Whence also we say that the holy men are just and that they are made pleasing to God after their sins not only through their merits but through the mercy of him to whom every creature is subject and stands in need of his mercy. Let heretics hear, who are lifted up by pride and say, “We have taken unto us horns by our own strength.” Let them listen to what Moab heard said to him: “We have heard the pride of Moab, he is exceedingly proud. ‘His haughtiness and his arrogance and his pride and the loftiness of his heart I know,’ says the Lord, ‘because his strength is not according to the loftiness thereof.’ ”

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
